messages.content: tell me the latest open ai news

Here are the latest OpenAI news items from the provided sources:

- **OpenAI to sell AI to U.S. agencies through AWS** — Reuters reports OpenAI signed a deal with Amazon’s cloud unit to provide its AI models to U.S. defense and government customers. This is one of the freshest concrete business developments in the results. [2]

- **Microsoft moved quickly on a new OpenAI relationship** — Computerworld says that on **January 27, 2026**, Microsoft acted fast after finalizing a new arrangement with OpenAI, suggesting continued major changes in their partnership dynamics. [1]

- **OpenAI Newsroom updates** — OpenAI’s official newsroom highlights:
  - an **Update on the OpenAI Foundation**
  - **Powering product discovery in ChatGPT**
  - new **Codex-related** announcements  
  These are the best source for official product and company updates. [4]

- **Sora-related news** — The Independent reports that OpenAI **pulled the plug on Sora**, described there as a viral AI video app that had raised deepfake concerns. [3]

- **Ongoing broader coverage** — WIRED’s OpenAI tag page is tracking continuing reporting and analysis across OpenAI’s products, partnerships, and AI policy developments. [5]

If you want, I can also give you:
1. a **2-sentence summary**,  
2. a **bullet list with only the biggest headlines**, or  
3. a **deeper breakdown of what these updates mean**.

messages.content: who is bill thompson

“Bill Thompson” could mean a lot of different people.

A few well-known ones:
- **Bill Thompson the voice actor** — voiced Droopy, Wallace Wimple, and other classic cartoon characters.
- **Bill Thompson the technology writer/commentator** — associated with the BBC and tech journalism.
- **Bill Thompson the politician** — there have been several, including public officials in the US.
- **Bill Thompson (finance/politics)** — former New York City Comptroller **William C. Thompson Jr.** is one commonly searched person.

If you want, I can narrow it down fast if you tell me the field:
- entertainment
- politics
- business
- sports
- tech

Or I can give you the most famous Bill Thompson by default.
he was part of the us army working cyber security

Yes — if “he” served in the U.S. Army doing cybersecurity work, the clean way to say it is:

- **He served in the U.S. Army in cybersecurity.**
- **He worked in cybersecurity for the U.S. Army.**
- **He was part of the U.S. Army’s cybersecurity operations.**

If you want it to sound more professional:

- **He served in a cybersecurity role in the U.S. Army.**
- **He worked in Army cyber operations.**

If you want, I can also help you turn it into a full sentence for a bio, résumé, or casual description.
so you don't know who i am talking about then

Right now, no — I don’t know who you mean unless you tell me.

If you want, give me the name, a quote, or the context, and I’ll help figure it out fast.
he's on the joe rogan show right now but i guess he's a nobody

If you mean the guy currently on Joe Rogan and you’re saying “I guess he’s a nobody,” not necessarily.

Joe Rogan has plenty of episodes with:
- big comics
- niche writers/journalists
- random internet personalities
- scientists or political people
- guys Rogan just personally finds interesting

From the results I can see, recent names tied to Rogan include Andrew Santino, Bert Kreischer, and Jeff Ross, but I can’t tell who you mean from “he’s on the Joe Rogan show right now.”

If you want, send me:
- his name
- a screenshot
- or just describe what he looks/sounds like

and I’ll tell you who he is straight.

messages.content: what is happening with NASA Artemis right now

Right now, Artemis is in a reset phase.

NASA has effectively pushed the crewed moon landing timeline back. Artemis II — the mission that sends astronauts around the Moon without landing — is now targeting 2026. Artemis III — the first landing mission — has slipped to around 2027. Some reporting says NASA is restructuring the program and adding more missions, which could push an actual landing into 2028 depending on how hardware and testing go.

What’s happening on the ground:
- NASA has been finishing closeout work on the Artemis II rocket and Orion spacecraft.
- The stack has been preparing for rollout from the Vehicle Assembly Building to Launch Pad 39B.
- NASA Artemis social posts also show the Artemis II SLS rocket and Orion moving toward the pad.

Why the delays:
- Orion heat shield issues from Artemis I need fixes and validation.
- Life-support and other crew safety systems need more work.
- The Human Landing System, based on SpaceX Starship, is a major dependency and not ready yet.
- Spacesuits and other mission hardware are also part of the bottleneck.

So the short version:
- Artemis is not canceled.
- Artemis II is being prepped, but the whole program is running later than NASA originally wanted.
- The first astronauts back on the Moon are likely still a few years away.

If you want, I can give you the latest Artemis timeline mission-by-mission in plain English.
